query: Can you suggest some literature that evaluates the ability of context-aware machine translation systems to handle discourse phenomena such as deixis and lexical cohesion?
criteria: evaluation of context-aware machine translation discourse deixis
context-aware machine translation lexical cohesion evaluation
discourse phenomena evaluation context-aware neural machine translation

title.value: When a Good Translation is Wrong in Context: Context-Aware Machine Translation Improves on Deixis, Ellipsis, and Lexical Cohesion

authors_year.value: Elena Voita, Rico Sennrich, Ivan Titov (2019)

source_or_doi.value: DOI: 10.18653/v1/p19-1116; Source: https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/p19-1116; OA: gold; Citations: 17

recommendation_reason.value: When a Good Translation is Wrong in Context: Context-Aware Machine Translation Improves on Deixis, Ellipsis, and Lexical Cohesion

supported_research_question.value: Though machine translation errors caused by the lack of context beyond one sentence have long been acknowledged, the development of context-aware NMT systems is hampered by several problems.

method.value: To address the first issue, we perform a human study on an English-Russian subtitles dataset and identify deixis, ellipsis and lexical cohesion as three main sources of inconsistency. We then create test sets targeting these phenomena.

main_conclusion.value: demonstrate major gains over a context-agnostic baseline on our new benchmarks

limitations.value: Firstly, standard metrics are not sensitive to improvements in consistency in document-level translations.

relevance_strength.value: Context-Aware Machine Translation Improves on Deixis, Ellipsis, and Lexical Cohesion

title.value: When Does Translation Require Context? A Data-driven, Multilingual Exploration

authors_year.value: Patrick Fernandes, Kayo Yin, Emmy Liu, André F. T. Martins, Graham Neubig (2023)

recommendation_reason.value: we develop the Multilingual Discourse-Aware (MUDA) benchmark, a series of taggers that identify and evaluate model performance on discourse phenomena

method.value: develop the Multilingual Discourse-Aware (MUDA) benchmark, a series of taggers

data_or_scenario.value: We release code and data for 14 language pairs

main_conclusion.value: common context-aware MT models make only marginal improvements over context-agnostic models

limitations.value: not in a fully systematic way

relevance_strength.value: target a small set of discourse phenomena during evaluation

title.value: Towards a Literary Machine Translation: The Role of Referential Cohesion

recommendation_reason.value: This paper examines how referential cohesion is expressed in literary and non-literary texts and how this cohesion affects translation.

supported_research_question.value: What is the role of textual features above the sentence level in advancing the machine translation of literature?

method.value: We first show in a corpus study on English that literary texts use more dense reference chains to express greater referential cohesion than news. We then compare the referential cohesion of machine versus human translations of Chinese literature and news.

main_conclusion.value: While human translators capture the greater referential cohesion of literature, Google translations perform less well at capturing literary cohesion.

limitations.value: Human translation of literary texts is an extremely time-and money-intensive task, but one that is a crucial element of the global system of transcultural literary exchange.

relevance_strength.value: Our results suggest that incorporating discourse features above the sentence level is an important direction for MT research if it is to be applied to literature.
